multiplechoiceresponse.label: In the COMPAS case study, as discussed in Lecture 2, imposing ε-demographic parity means:

choicegroup.choice: Both false positive and false negative rates must match exactly across groups.
Group membership is dropped from the model to ensure fairness.
The proportion of “high risk” predictions is constrained to be similar across groups.
The dataset is balanced by resampling minority defendants to match majority counts.

div.p: ε-demographic parity is defined as requiring that the difference in positive decision rates between groups is at most ε. In the COMPAS setting, the positive outcome corresponds to being labeled “high risk.” This constraint does not require equal error rates, removal of group attributes, or resampling the data. Instead, it directly limits disparities in the fraction of positive predictions across groups.
